data.title: Bitget Onchain Trading (Onchain) Launches MASK
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Bitget Onchain Trading (Onchain) has launched Solana ecosystem's MEME token MASK. Users can start trading in the on-chain trading section. Bitget Onchain Trading (Onchain) aims to seamlessly connect CEX and DEX to provide users with a more convenient, efficient and secure on-chain trading experience. Users can directly use Bitget spot accounts (USDT/USDC) to trade popular assets on the chain. Currently, popular public chains such as Solana (SOL), BNB Smart Chain (BSC) and Base are supported.

data.title: Gracy Chen was interviewed by CNBC and talked about crypto catalysts, institutional interest and Bitget's Asian layout
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Bitget CEO Gracy Chen was interviewed by CNBC during Bitcoin2025. In the interview, she shared her views on the catalysts for the next round of cryptocurrency, the level of participation of institutional investors, and Bitget's strategic planning in the Asian market.
When talking about Bitget's launch of USD1, Gracy said: "USD1, a stablecoin issued by WLF, was launched. On the one hand, it is because of user demand, and on the other hand, we also see that WLF is promoting its ecological cooperation, and may have deeper cooperation with us in the future."

data.title: Crypto News: SEC Dismisses Case Against Binance, Signaling Shift Toward Regulatory Clarity in U.S. Crypto Policy
data.description:
In a pivotal move for the cryptocurrency industry, the United States Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has officially dismissed its long-standing case against Binance. The decision marks a turning point in U. S. regulatory posture, reinforcing a global narrative that favors clearer frameworks over enforcement-first approaches
source: https://www.binance.com/en/square/post/24976748606914?utm_source=BinanceNewsRSS

data.title: Binance Alpha Lists Taiko (TAIKO) With Airdrop for Eligible Users
data.description:
Binance Alpha has officially listed Taiko (TAIKO), an open-source, Ethereum-equivalent ZK-rollup that enhances scalability while maintaining Ethereum compatibility. As part of the listing celebration, eligible Binance users holding at least 205 Alpha Points can claim an exclusive airdrop of 130 TAIKO tokens starting May 31 at 14:00 (UTC+8) via the Alpha event page. Taiko is designed to deliver a decentralized, permissionless Layer 2 solution using zero-knowledge proofs, enabling high throughput, low fees, and full Ethereum compatibility. As a Type-1 ZK-EVM, Taiko allows developers to deploy Ethereum-based applications without modifications, further enhancing Ethereum’s scalability and user experience
source: https://www.binance.com/en/square/post/24976778636569?utm_source=BinanceNewsRSS

data.title: Crypto News: SEC Dismisses Case Against Binance, Signaling Shift Toward Regulatory Clarity in U.S. Crypto Policy
data.description: In a pivotal move for the cryptocurrency industry, the United States Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has officially dismissed its long-standing case against Binance. The decision marks a turning point in U.S. regulatory posture, reinforcing a global narrative that favors clearer frameworks over enforcement-first approaches. Industry observers say the outcome could spark renewed confidence across global markets and accelerate progress toward more structured crypto regulation.SEC Case Dismissal Marks Shift in U.S. Crypto StrategyThe lawsuit’s dismissal comes at a time when the U.S. regulatory landscape is undergoing reevaluation, with bipartisan crypto legislation progressing in Congress and growing calls for regulatory reform. Binance, while not serving U.S. users directly, has faced scrutiny over its global operations and compliance standards. The case against the exchange had become a focal point for the broader industry debate over the SEC's regulatory approach.The dismissal is being interpreted by many as an indication that U.S. regulators may be pivoting from litigation-led strategy to one focused on long-term industry engagement and rulemaking.“This is a signal that the era of regulatory ambiguity and litigation as policy may be winding down,” noted one regulatory analyst. “It gives regulators outside the U.S. a clearer picture of Washington’s evolving stance on digital assets.”Binance’s Global Compliance and Regulatory StandingIn the wake of the dismissal, Binance emphasized its regulatory footprint and efforts to build a robust global compliance framework. The company now holds 21 regulatory approvals across multiple jurisdictions, including several G7 nations, and operates a 650+ person compliance team with an active financial crimes unit.In 2024 alone, Binance responded to nearly 65,000 law enforcement requests and supported over 14,000 officials globally — numbers the exchange says reflect its commitment to responsible operations.“This decision validates our long-standing efforts to meet and exceed global compliance standards,” a Binance spokesperson said.Implications for the U.S. and Global Crypto EcosystemThe dismissal of the SEC’s case has wider implications beyond Binance. Analysts suggest it could catalyze regulatory clarity, boost investor confidence, and unlock opportunities for U.S.-led innovation in blockchain technology and decentralized finance.Observers also expect a ripple effect on global regulators, particularly in the European Union and the UK, where policy direction often mirrors the U.S.“When the U.S. takes a step forward, others follow,” said a policy expert. “This may encourage jurisdictions that have held back to advance their own crypto frameworks.”A New Chapter for Crypto Regulation?While the lawsuit’s dismissal doesn’t end the conversation on regulatory oversight, it does offer a moment to reframe it. Industry leaders are calling for this momentum to be used to develop a balanced, transparent, and collaborative framework that protects consumers without hampering innovation.With the legal overhang lifted, exchanges, developers, and institutions alike may feel more confident engaging with U.S. regulators — and vice versa.The SEC’s decision to dismiss its case against Binance marks more than a legal resolution — it reflects a broader realignment in how the United States approaches digital assets. As the regulatory tone shifts from punitive to constructive, the global crypto ecosystem may enter a new phase of adoption, collaboration, and growth.

data.title: Binance Alpha launches TAIKO, users with 205 Alpha points can receive 130 TAIKO airdrops
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Binance Alpha announced the launch of TAIKO. Qualified Binance users with at least 205 Alpha points can claim an airdrop of 130 TAIKO tokens on the Alpha event page starting at 6:00 UTC on May 31, 2025 (14:00 Beijing time). Claiming the TAIKO airdrop will consume 15 Binance Alpha points, and users must confirm the claim on the Alpha event page within 24 hours, otherwise it will be deemed to have given up the claim.
